revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24644718927|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Daikai-jinja]][[ja:大海神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Daikai Shrine | native_name = 大海神社 | image = Daikai-jinja, heiden-1.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Daikai Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.613928|135.493209}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi|az|Vatatsumi|de|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|es|Watatsumi|fa|واتاتسومی|fr|Watatsumi|hu|Vatacumi|ja|ワタツミ|ko|와타쓰미|mni|ꯋꯇꯠꯁꯨꯃꯤ|pl|Watatsumi|ru|Ватацуми|simple|Watatsumi|uk|Вататсумі|zh|綿津見|qid=Q1474187|1=Watatsumi}} | website = {{Official website|http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/grounds/sessya.html#anchor01}} }} {{nihongo|'''Daikai Shrine'''|大海神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q9610964|ltq=Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q85878469|ltq=Q85878469|qid=Q85878469|1=Keidai-Sessha}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11498550|ltq=Q11498550|qid=Q11498550|1=List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 12 ** P155: {{ill|Q135098896|ltq=Q135098896|qid=Q135098896|1=Nunotahimeno- Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Tameno Shrine|lt=Tameno Shrine|qid=Q135098897|1=Tameno Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q79370444|ltq=Q79370444|qid=Q79370444|1=Sumiyoshi}} * {{ill|Q864163|ltq=Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi district|lt=Sumiyoshi district|ja|住吉郡|zh|住吉郡|qid=Q10885872|1=Sumiyoshi district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.613928|135.493209}} == official website (P856) == * http://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/grounds/sessya.html#anchor01 ** Language of work: {{ill|Japanese language|lt=Japanese|ab|Аиапон бызшәа|ace|Bahsa Jeupang|ady|Японыбзэ|af|Japannees|als|Japanische Sprache|ami|Sowal no dipong|am|ጃፓንኛ|ang|Iapanisc sprǣc|anp|जापानी भाषा|an|Idioma chaponés|arc|ܠܫܢܐ ܝܦܢܝܐ|ar|اللغة اليابانية|arbooks|يابانية|ary|جاپونية|arz|لغه يابانى|ast|Xaponés|as|জাপানী дыл|ty|Reo Tāpōnē|udm|Япон кыл|ug|ياپونچە|uk|Японська мова|ukbooks|Японська мова|ur|جاپانی زبان|uz|Yapon tili|vec|Łéngua japoneza|vep|Japonan kel'|vi|Tiếng Nhật|viquote|Tiếng Nhật|vivoyage|Sổ tay tiếng Nhật|vo|Yapänapük|war|Hinapon|wa|Djaponès (lingaedje)|functions|Z1830|wuu|日本闲话|xal|Нипонмудн келн|xmf|იაპონური ნინა|yi|יאפאניש|yo|Èdè Japaní|za|Vah Nyiedbonj|zgh|ⵜⵓⵜⵍⴰⵢⵜ ⵜⴰⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏⵜ|zh_classical|日語|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún-gí|zh_yue|日文|zh|日语|zhbooks|日语|zhvoyage|日语会话手册|zu|IsiJapani|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * Daikai-jinja, heiden-1.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Watatsumi|lt=Watatsumi|az|Vatatsumi|de|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|es|Watatsumi|fa|واتاتسومی|fr|Watatsumi|hu|Vatacumi|ja|ワタツミ|ko|와타쓰미|mni|ꯋꯇꯠꯁꯨꯃꯤ|pl|Watatsumi|ru|Ватацуми|simple|Watatsumi|uk|Вататсумі|zh|綿津見|qid=Q1474187|1=Watatsumi}} * {{ill|Q2329261|ltq=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|1=Toyotama-hime}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181032 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == heritage designation (P1435) == * {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|lt=Important Cultural Property of Japan|de|Wichtiges Kulturgut Japans|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|es|Bien Cultural Importante de Japón|fi|Japanin tärkeä kulttuuriomaisuus|fr|Bien culturel important|id|Properti Kebudayaan Penting (Jepang)|it|Bene culturale importante (Giappone)|ja|重要文化財|ko|일본의 중요문화재|ro|Patrimoniu cultural important al Japoniei|simple|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|th|ทรัพย์สินทางวัฒนธรรมที่สำคัญ (ญี่ปุ่น)|tr|Önemli Kültürel Varlık (Japonya)|uk|Важливі культурні пам'ятки Японії|zh|重要文化財|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q3503635|ltq=Q3503635|qid=Q3503635|1=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * だいかいじんじゃ == location (P276) == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|de|Sumiyoshi-Taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|es|Sumiyoshi Taisha|fa|سومییوشی-تایشا|fi|Sumiyoshi-taisha|fr|Sumiyoshi-taisha|it|Grande santuario di Sumiyoshi|ja|住吉大社|ko|스미요시 대사|la|Sumiyoshi-taisha|my|ဆုမိရောရှိနန်းကွန်းနန်းရင်း|pt|Sumiyoshi taisha|ru|Сумиёси-тайся|sl|Veliko svetišče Sumijoši-taiša|th|ศาลเจ้าซูมิโยชิ|tr|Sumiyoshi-taisha|uk|Сумійосі тайся|zh|住吉大社|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 19729222 == Commons category (P373) == * Daikai-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Q135018062|ltq=Q135018062|qid=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府大阪市住吉区住吉2-9-89（住吉大社境内）', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Daikai-jinja, torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Daikai-jinja}} [[JA:大海神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11437478}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Daikai Shrine |Image = [[File:Daikai-jinja, heiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Heiden (National {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward|Q985702|qid=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}} Sumiyoshi 2-9-89 (within the precincts of {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}) |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = Toyotama-hiko-no-Mikoto
Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Auxiliary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} |Established = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|qid=Q2925|1=October 13}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-zukuri|Q3503635|qid=Q3503635|1=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Daikai Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Daikai-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Daikai Shrine''' (だいかいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Sumiyoshi, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward|Q985702|qid=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} and is currently an auxiliary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following two kami{{Sfn|Sumiyoshi Taisha Ryakki|1999|p=25}}: * {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|qid=Q1474187|lt=Toyotama-hiko-no-Mikoto|1=Watatsumi}} (Toyotama-hiko-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto) In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine’s enshrined deities are recorded as two seats{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. In the same register, an annotation reads “originally named Tsumori Yasuhito no Kami,” but in *{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmei-chō|Q11571092|qid=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}* and *{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|qid=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}*, this “Tsumori Yasuhito no Kami” is emended to “Tsumori Ujibito no Kami”{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine (Deities)|2000}}. Since then, a theory has been known that interprets this shrine as the clan deity of the {{ill|lt=Tsumori clan|Q11555993|qid=Q11555993|1=Tsumori clan}} (originally Tsumori no Muraji, later Tsumori no Sukune), the clan that served {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine (Deities)|2000}}{{Sfn|Tsumori clan (ancient clan)|2010}}{{Sfn|Okada Seishi|2011}}. However, in the *{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}*, Tsumori no Sukune is said to share an ancestor with the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|qid=Q11465311|lt=Owari no Sukune|1=Owari clan}}, being a descendant of {{ill|lt=Ame no Hoakari no Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|lt=Hoakari no Mikoto (Ame no Hoakari)|1=Amenohoakari}}; this differs from the sea deity (Watatsumi no Kami / Toyotama-hiko), who is the ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Azumi clan|Q3377806|qid=Q3377806|1=Azumi people}}. Another theory identifies the deity described in the *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|Q11381815|qid=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}* as “Tsumori Yasuhito no Kami, two offerings, called Sea Deity” with this shrine, and takes the *Engishiki* entry literally as “Tsumori Yasuhito no Kami”{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine (Deities)|2000}}{{Sfn|Mayumi Tsunetada|2003|pp=152–153}}. Under this interpretation, “Yasuhito no Kami” and “Yasuhito no Kami” are understood to mean an {{ill|lt=arahitogami|Q521834|qid=Q521834|1=Arahitogami}} or the meaning of {{ill|lt=Arahito Shrine|Q85882377|qid=Q85882377|1=Arahito Shrine}}—the head shrine of the {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Three Deities|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} at {{ill|lt=Nakahara River|Q1206001|qid=Q1206001|lt=Nakahara|1=Nakagawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Chikuzen Province|Q1047664|qid=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen|1=Chikuzen Province}} (the former original shrine of {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka City)|Q29682|qid=Q29682|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)}} in Chikuzen){{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine (Deities)|2000}}{{Sfn|Mayumi Tsunetada|2003|pp=152–153}}. In any case, the connection with the Tsumori clan is deep; in the *Sumiyoshi Matsuba Taiki*, it is said that the legitimate son of Tezuri Ashi no Sukune ({{ill|lt=Tamo-mi no Sukune|Q20041346|qid=Q20041346|1=Tamo-mi no Sukune}}, progenitor of the Tsumori clan) served Daikai Shrine as the Great Steward{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. Although the current enshrined deities are the two mentioned above, the *Sumiyoshi Matsuba Taiki* treats Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto as an associated deity{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. As alternative theories, *{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|qid=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}* lists Ōwatatsumi-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime (Hyūga myth)|Q3082751|qid=Q3082751|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} as the two deities, while *Jingi Shiryō* lists three deities: {{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}, Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|qid=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|1=Hoori}}{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. === History === In a document from the first year of {{ill|lt=Daido (Japanese era)|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|qid=Q30021|1=806}}) contained in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kakuchokufushō|Q11502312|qid=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*, it is recorded that the then “Sumiyoshi Tarumi Deity” was assigned 22 households as {{ill|lt=kannabe|Q11589711|qid=Q11589711|lt=shrine households|1=Kanbe (social class)}}. Of these, two households were assigned from {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} by an order dated December 29 of the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō Hōji|Q1346307|qid=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} ({{ill|lt=759|Q30532|qid=Q30532|1=759}}), and one household from {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|qid=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}} by an order dated September 7 of the first year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō Jingō|Q1373181|qid=Q1373181|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} ({{ill|lt=765|Q31216|qid=Q31216|1=765}}). There is a theory that identifies this “Sumiyoshi Tarumi Deity” with Daikai Shrine{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}} (another theory identifies it with {{ill|lt=Tarumi Shrine (Suita City)|Q11425829|qid=Q11425829|lt=Tarumi Shrine|1=Tarumi Shrine (Q11425829)}} in Suita CityEisawa Eitarō, “Tarumi Shrine,” *Shikinaisha Research Report*, Vol. 5, edited by the Shikinaisha Research Association, Kōgakkan University Press, 1977.*Newly Revised History of Osaka City, Main Text, Vol. 1*, Osaka City, 1988, pp. 41–42.). Another theory identifies the “two seats of Sea Deities” mentioned in the section on extraordinary festivals in the *Engishiki*, and the “Sumiyoshi Sea Deity” mentioned in the entry for July 5 of the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q24318|qid=Q24318|1=948}} ({{ill|lt=948|Q24318|qid=Q24318|1=948}}) in the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Kiraku|Q1053542|qid=Q1053542|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}*, with this shrine, holding that it participated in the {{ill|lt=Yasoshima Festival|Q24870749|qid=Q24870749|1=Yasojima Festival}} together with the Sumiyoshi deities{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. In the *Engishiki* *Jinmyōchō* compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Engi (era)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), it is recorded under {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi District|Q10885872|qid=Q10885872|1=Sumiyoshi district}} of {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} as “Daikai Shrine, two seats, minor, originally named Tsumori Yasuhito no Kami,” and the two seats are listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. Thereafter, it continued as an auxiliary shrine of Sumiyoshi Taisha, and it is said that in the past, when the main sanctuary of Sumiyoshi Taisha underwent its periodic reconstruction, the buildings of this shrine were also rebuilt{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. === Precincts === [[File:Daikai-jinja, shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine buildings}}{{small|Heiden on the left, main hall at the back right. A watariden stands between the main hall and the heiden.}}]] The shrine buildings are located toward the northern side of the Sumiyoshi Taisha precincts. The '''main hall''' dates from the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, having been constructed in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|qid=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|lt=1708|Q6846|qid=Q6846|1=1708}}). Like the main sanctuary of Sumiyoshi Taisha (constructed in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Bunka (era)|Q1373146|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} ({{ill|lt=1810|Q6922|qid=Q6922|1=1810}}), a National Treasure), it is built in the {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-zukuri|Q3503635|qid=Q3503635|1=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} style, but it is older than the main sanctuary and, like it, faces west toward the sea. The roof is {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. It is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. In front of the main hall stands the '''watariden''' (connecting hall), followed by the '''heiden'''; both were also constructed in the mid-Edo period, in the 5th year of Hōei (1708), like the main hall. The watariden is a two-bay by one-bay structure with double-eaved roofing, covered in hiwadabuki. The heiden is in the wari-haiden style, three bays wide and two bays deep, with a gabled roof, also covered in hiwadabuki. Compared with the main precincts, the construction is simpler{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. The watariden and heiden together are designated as a single National Important Cultural Property. The '''west gate''' standing at the entrance to the precincts in front of the heiden dates from the early Edo period. It is a four-post gate with a gabled roof covered in hon-kawara tiles. It is designated as a National Important Cultural Property. Outside the west gate, the terrain forms a cliff, and it is said that the sea once spread out below{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}. It is also said that there was once a “Taka-haiden” to the southwest of the shrine buildings, but it has now been lost{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine Two Seats (Shikinaisha)|1977}}{{Sfn|Daikai Shrine (Deities)|2000}}. In addition to the above, there is a well called “Tama-no-i” in front of the shrine, which is said to be the place where Yamasachihiko submerged the tide-jewel he received from the Sea Deity{{Sfn|Sumiyoshi Taisha Ryakki|1999|p=25}}{{Sfn|Sumiyoshi Taisha (Gakuseisha)|2000|pp=204–205}}. Nearby also stands Shiga Shrine, another auxiliary shrine within the precincts of Sumiyoshi Taisha. File:Daikai-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (National Important Cultural Property) File:Daikai-jinja, nishimon.jpg|West gate (National Important Cultural Property) File:Daikai-jinja, Tama-no-i-2.jpg|Tama-no-i well === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (nationally designated) === * Main hall (with attached sacred fence and gate) (structure) *: Built in the mid-Edo period, in the 5th year of Hōei (1708). Designated on May 26, {{ill|lt=1964|Q11540|qid=Q11540|1=1008 La Paz}}{{National Cultural Properties Database|102|2094|Main Hall of Daikai Shrine, Auxiliary Shrine of Sumiyoshi Taisha}}. * Of the Important Cultural Property “Sumiyoshi Taisha, 13 buildings,” the following two structures (structures): ** Heiden and Watariden **: Built in the mid-Edo period, in the 5th year of Hōei (1708). Designated on December 24, {{ill|lt=2010|Q19884|qid=Q19884|1=TIE fighter}}{{National Cultural Properties Database|102|00004494|Heiden and Watariden of Daikai Shrine, Auxiliary Shrine}}. ** West gate **: Built in the early Edo period. Designated on December 24, {{ill|lt=2010|Q19884|qid=Q19884|1=TIE fighter}}{{National Cultural Properties Database|102|00004495|West Gate of Daikai Shrine, Auxiliary Shrine}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Publications edited by Sumiyoshi Taisha ** {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Office|year=1999|title=Sumiyoshi Taisha Ryakki, Revised Ancient History of Shrines|publisher={{ill|lt=Gakuseisha|Q11448819|qid=Q11448819|1=Gakuseisha}}|isbn=978-4311203022|pages=72–109|ref={{Harvid|Okada Seishi|2011}}}} === Related items === *
